title: Summary of affiliations and interests: Scientific Advisory Board on Vaping Products - Canada.ca
content:
## Summary of affiliations and interests: Scientific Advisory Board on Vaping Products
In accordance with Health Canada Policy on External Advisory Bodies (2011),  all members were asked to disclose:
- Direct financial interests
- Indirect financial interests
- Intellectual interests
- Any other relevant affiliations or interests
At every meeting, members confirm or update their stated affiliations and interests. No members were prohibited from participating on the basis of their disclosures.

## 1. Direct financial interests
- Current employment, investments in companies, partnerships, equity, royalties, joint ventures, trusts, real property, stocks, shares or bonds, with the regulated industry.

## 2. Indirect financial interests
- Within the past ten years, payment from regulated industry for work done or being done, including past employment, contracts or consulting; or financial support including research support, personal education grants, contributions, fellowships, sponsorships, and honoraria.
- Within the past ten years, materials, discounted products, gifts, or other benefits, or attendance at meetings where all or part of the travel and accommodation costs were provided by a party that has an interest in the mandate of the advisory body.
- Within the last ten years, grants or other funding to any of the organizations where you are currently employed or participate in internal decision making from a party that has an interest in the mandate of the advisory body.

## 3. Intellectual interests
- Within the last ten years, any formal advice or opinion to industry, a government organization or a non-government organization on a matter of relevance to the mandate of the advisory body.
- Within the last ten years, any published or publicly stated point of view on issues of relevance to the board's mandate.
- Current professional or volunteer affiliations such as membership of professional societies, lobbying, public interest or advocacy groups, of relevance to the board.
- Any other affiliations and interests or potential circumstances that might give a well-informed member of the public reasonable grounds for concern regarding the integrity and objectivity of your participation.

## Summary of affiliations and interests

### Steven J. Hoffman, J.D., Ph.D., LL.D.

#### Summary of responses
- Served as an  expert witness for the Government of Canada in a legal challenge related to  vaping.
- Affiliations  with: Canadian  Institutes of Health Research
- Canadian  Institutes of Health Research

### Carolyn Baglole, Ph.D., MSc

#### Summary of responses
- Public  statements in the form of publications, presentations, and lectures.
- Affiliations  with: Quebec  Respiratory Health Network: Environment/Genetics/Cancer Axis American  Thoracic Society Society of  Toxicology
- Quebec  Respiratory Health Network: Environment/Genetics/Cancer Axis
- American  Thoracic Society
- Society of  Toxicology

### Judith G. Bartlett, C.M., MD, MSc, CCFP

#### Summary of responses
- Affiliations  with: Canadian Medical Association Doctors Manitoba College of Family Physicians of Canada
- Canadian Medical Association
- College of Family Physicians of Canada

#### Summary of responses

- Affiliations  with: Canadian  Paediatric Society
- Canadian  Paediatric Society

### Joanna Cohen, Ph.D., MHSc

#### Summary of responses
- Public  statements in the form of presentations, publications, and reports.

### Mark Eisenberg, MD, MPH, FACC, FAHA, FCAHS

#### Summary of responses

- Public  statements in the form of publications.

### Dr. Maciej L. Goniewicz, Ph.D., PharmD

#### Summary of responses
- Past member  of an advisory board for a pharmaceutical company.
- Provided  advice to governmental agencies/organizations related to health effects of  vaping products and heated tobacco products.
- Public  statements in the form of publications and reports.
- Affiliations  with: Society for  Research on Nicotine and Tobacco Society of  Toxicology American  Association for Cancer Research International  Association for the Study of Lung Cancer
- Society for  Research on Nicotine and Tobacco
- Society of  Toxicology
- American  Association for Cancer Research
- International  Association for the Study of Lung Cancer

### David Hammond, Ph.D., M.Sc.

#### Summary of responses
- Served as a  paid expert witness in several legal challenges related to tobacco control.
- Travel  reimbursements on behalf of public health non-governmental organizations for  presentations related to the topics of smoking and vaping.
- Public  statements in the form of presentations, publications, media interviews, and  testimony.
- Affiliations  with: Society for  Research on Nicotine and Tobacco Heart &  Stroke Foundation Cancer Care  Ontario
- Society for  Research on Nicotine and Tobacco
- Heart &  Stroke Foundation
- Cancer Care  Ontario

### Jamie Hartmann-Boyce, MA, DPhil

#### Summary of responses
- Advice  provided to government departments and bodies on electronic cigarettes.
- Public  statements in the form of publications, reports, broadcast and print media.
- Affiliations  with: Society for  Research on Nicotine and Tobacco
- Society for  Research on Nicotine and Tobacco

### Milan Khara MBChB, CCFP, Dip. ABAM

#### Summary of responses

#### Summary of responses
- Served as a  paid consultant for the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention and the  Virginia Foundation for Healthy Youth.
- Public  statements in the form of publications and letters prepared for government  officials and legislative hearings.

#### Summary of responses
